title: Grammitis cretata
taxonomy: Grammitis cretata is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a member of the genus Grammitis, which includes various species of ferns. The plant is native to tropical regions, primarily in Central and South America. The classification of Grammitis cretata places it within the division Pteridophyta and the phylum Pteridophyta. Further classification includes the kingdom Plantae and the group Tracheophyta.

intro: Grammitis cretata, also known as Cretan Grammitis, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Grammitis cretata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Grammitis cretata is often made into a tea or infusion, or used as a tincture, to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
